title: Prompt Engineering for Better AI Results

learning_objectives: Learn how to improve prompts through structure and iteration.

blocks.title: Week 7 Focus
blocks.content: Students turn vague prompts into useful instructions with audience, context, and format.

items.label: Context
items.description: Explain the situation or task clearly.

items.label: Audience
items.description: Tell the model who the output is for.

items.label: Format
items.description: Specify the structure you want back.

items.label: Role
items.description: Optionally set the role or perspective for the model.

items.label: Task
items.description: Describe the exact action you want.

items.label: Constraints
items.description: Limit length, tone, scope, or content.

items.label: Output Format
items.description: Ask for bullets, tables, quiz questions, or summaries.

blocks.title: Key Habit
blocks.content: Students should expect to refine prompts instead of waiting for one perfect first answer.

activity.title: Prompt Makeover Challenge
activity.instructions: Rewrite a weak prompt to include context, audience, and output format.
activity.success_criteria: Produce a clearer prompt with at least three improvements.

questions.prompt: What usually makes a prompt more useful?
options: Adding audience, context, and output format
questions.explanation: Specific context and output expectations usually improve the result.

options: Create a 5-question beginner quiz on machine learning with answers
questions.explanation: The stronger prompt gives a clear task, audience level, and output format.

questions.prompt: Why is iteration important in prompting?
options: Because the first answer is always perfect
Because prompts often improve through revision
Because shorter prompts are always best
questions.explanation: Prompting is often an iterative process of clarifying and improving the instruction.

reflection.prompt: Take a weak prompt from your own work or study and explain how you would improve it.
reflection.placeholder: I would add the audience, the exact task, tone, and output format so the AI knows what good looks like.

teacher_notes: Start with a weak prompt and improve it publicly in steps.
Make students justify why each extra detail improves the result.
